title: Chapter 2: A Very Apocalyptic Shopping Spree
content: Roxanne found Lena in the second-floor studio, huddled behind a stack of canvases with two other students.

Lena's face was streaked with charcoal dust and tears. Her hands were shaking so badly she couldn't hold her sketchbook steady. When she saw Roxanne walk through the door — alive, walking, covered in dust but clearly not dead — she let out a sound that was half-sob, half-laugh.
"Roxy! Oh my god, I thought — Marcus said —"
"Marcus said a lot of things." Roxanne's voice was flat. "Marcus is an asshole. We're not talking about Marcus."
She pulled Lena to her feet. The other two students — a nervous-looking guy in a hoodie and a girl with bright pink hair that was now drooping sadly — stared at her with wide eyes.
"Is it true?" the pink-haired girl whispered. "Jake said you — you bought the thing. The chainsaw monster. He said it's following you now."
"Employee of the month," Roxanne said. "Works for me now. One hundred year contract. Good benefits."
Nobody laughed. The apocalypse had apparently killed everyone's sense of humor.

"Look." Roxanne pulled out the black card. It pulsed faintly in the dim light, the violet glow casting strange shadows across her face. "I don't fully understand what this is. But I know what it can do."
She held it up.
"I'm going to buy this entire campus. And everyone inside it is going to be safe."
She didn't know how to buy a campus. The card didn't come with an instruction manual.
But she'd watched enough TikTok to understand that if you wanted something, you asked for it directly.

"Card," she said, walking to the window that overlooked the main quad. "I want to buy Blackwood University. All of it. The buildings, the grounds, the underground tunnels, the weird chapel basement — everything. How much?"
The card pulsed.
Numbers appeared in front of her eyes, floating in the air like augmented reality.

*BLACKWOOD UNIVERSITY — COMPLETE TERRITORY*
*— Main Campus: 45 acres*
*— 12 Academic Buildings*
*— 4 Dormitory Blocks*
*— Underground Network (including Hollow Cathedral annex)*
*— Current Entity Occupancy: 47 hostile manifestations*

*BASE PRICE: 8,000,000 Obols*
*— Entity Clearance Fee (47 entities): +470,000 Obols*
*— Ley Node Premium (Hollow Cathedral): +2,000,000 Obols*
*TOTAL: 10,470,000 Obols*
Roxanne stared.
Eight million. Plus two million for the creepy basement cathedral.
She didn't have eight million of anything. She had twenty-eight dollars and a maxed-out credit card that was probably worthless now.
But the card in her hand said *Balance: ∞.*
"Okay," she said. "That's surprisingly reasonable for a whole university. Purchase."
The card pulsed. Warm. Then hot.
*APPROVED. PURCHASE COMPLETE.*

*BLACKWOOD UNIVERSITY IS NOW REGISTERED UNDER: ROXANNE VANCE*
*— All hostile entities on grounds are now trespassing*
*— Territorial wards active*
*— Nether-Grid deed filed*

For a moment, nothing happened.
Then the ground shook.
A deep, resonant hum rolled through the ground like a subway train passing beneath the building. The violet veins in the sky flickered. And then —
Roxanne felt it. A shift in the air. Like the campus had just... taken a breath.
Outside the window, the monsters in the quad stopped moving.
There were half a dozen of them — twisted things that looked like humans stretched too thin, with too many joints and too few facial features. They had been pacing aimlessly, waiting for prey.
Now they stood still.
One of them turned its head — way too far, like an owl — and looked directly at the Fine Arts building.
"Uh, Roxy?" Lena's voice cracked. "They're looking at us."
"They're looking at the building," Roxanne corrected. "The building is mine now. They're trespassing."
She opened the window.
"Hey!" she called down. "You're on my property!"
Everyone in the room stared at her like she'd lost her mind.
"Roxy, what are you —"
"I'm asserting dominance. It's a real estate thing."
The creatures below tilted their heads in unison. For a long, tense moment, nothing happened.
Then the ground beneath them split open.

Not a crack. A mouth. The asphalt parted like lips, revealing a darkness that didn't just lack light — it *ate* light. The creatures fell through before they could react, their stretched bodies swallowed whole. The ground sealed.
Silence.
The quad was empty.
Roxanne turned back to the room.
"So," she said, pocketing the card. "Who wants to see my new campus?"
The tour was surreal.

Every building on campus now displayed a price tag in Roxanne's eldritch vision — floating numbers above doors, windows, even individual rooms. The library was worth 1.2 million Obols. The science block, 2.1 million. The dormitories varied by how many monsters were currently squatting in them.
She walked through the campus with Lena, the Executioner dragging its chainsaw behind them, and began taking inventory.

"West Hall has three creepers on the second floor," Roxanne said, reading the floating text above the building. "Eviction cost: 30,000 Obols. Worth it — that's the building with the good studio lighting."
"You can afford to just — kick them out?" Lena asked.
"I can afford to buy them and tell them to leave. Same result."

She cleared West Hall in under a minute. The three entities — vaguely humanoid, made of what looked like solidified smoke — materialized on the lawn, looked confused, and dissolved into the violet sky.

By the time they reached the main quad again, Roxanne had cleared four buildings, checked the cafeteria (infested with something called "Spore Crawlers" — purchase-and-clear: 22,000 Obols), and marked the humanities block as a potential safe-dormitory zone.
"This is insane," Lena said, sketching as she walked. Charcoal flying. "You're just — buying everything."
"It's called investment."
"It's called having infinite money in an economy where money is the only thing that matters."
Roxanne stopped.
"Huh. That's actually a good point. I should see what else the market has."
She held up the black card and focused on the campus as a whole.
*SHOW ME WHAT ELSE IS FOR SALE IN THIS REGION.*
The card pulsed. A translucent screen unfolded in the air before her, lines of violet text scrolling like a stock ticker:

*— Blackwood University: OWNED*
*— East District (Commercial Zone): 12M Obols*
*— St. Mary's Hospital: 18M Obols (Entity Clearance Required)*
*— Municipal Infrastructure (Power/Water Grid): 40M Obols*
*— Regional Ley Node Rights: 150M Obols*
*— Protection Contract (Local Survivor Alliance): NEGOTIABLE*
Her breath caught.
"Roxy?"
"Lena. I think I just found my next project."
She tapped the *Protection Contract* option.

*SALVATION COALITION — TERRITORY PROTECTION SUBCONTRACT*
*— Coverage: Eastern Sector*
*— Current Holder: Unknown*
*— Buyout Price: 250,000 Obols*
*— Status: Active*
"The Salvation Coalition has a protection contract," Roxanne murmured. "Someone's paying for coverage. But who? And who's collecting?"
She filed the information away and dismissed the screen.
"We need somewhere safe to sleep tonight," she said. "Let's find the nicest dormitory and claim it."

Two miles away, in the fortified engineering building that served as the Salvation Coalition's headquarters, a man in a military surplus jacket stared at a dead radio and tried very hard not to panic.

His name was Colonel Voss. He'd been a real estate developer before the apocalypse. The "Colonel" was a self-appointed title. But he had guns, he had a building, and he had forty-three people who believed his promises.
That was enough to run a protection racket.

"The Nether-Grid feed just updated," said a woman at a laptop that was somehow still running — powered by a generator they'd found in the engineering basement. "Blackwood University just changed ownership."
"Changed ownership?" Voss frowned. "To who?"
The woman's face went pale.
"To someone named... Roxanne Vance. The deed says 'Purchased in Full. No liens.'"
"That's impossible. That campus is crawling with —"
"I know. But the feed says it's been cleared. All hostile entities on grounds have been evicted. The whole campus is now a neutral zone."
Voss stared at her.
"Find out who this Roxanne Vance is," he said slowly. "And find out how she paid for it."
Across the quad from the Fine Arts building, Marcus Chen sat on a curb with a monster standing three feet behind him.
The Executioner hadn't moved in three hours. It just stood there. Chainsaw silent. Rusted mask fixed on the back of his head.
Every time Marcus tried to stand, the chainsaw revved once.

He wasn't sure if it would kill him if he ran. He wasn't sure if it would stop following him if he stayed. He wasn't sure of anything except that Roxanne — his quiet, art-obsessed, credit-card-drowning girlfriend — had just bought a supernatural horror with infinite money and turned it into his personal stalker.
"This is insane," he whispered.
The Executioner said nothing.

But somewhere in the Fine Arts building, Roxanne had just found the dean's old office — leather chair, mahogany desk, a minifridge with three bottles of sparkling water — and was settling in for the night.
She looked out the window at the violet sky, at the campus that was now hers, at the strange future stretching out before her.
"Not bad," she said to no one. "Not bad at all."
Somewhere in the depths of the Hollow Cathedral beneath her new property, something ancient stirred in its sleep. The Black Card's activation had sent a ripple through the Nether-Grid.
And the Bound God felt it.
*End of Chapter 2*
